item.title: Nurse union, Ebola, no-bid contracts
item.ns0:title: 12-Year-Old Boy and 40-Year-Old Man Arraigned in Court Following Tragic Shooting of Eight-Year-Old in Bimini — Thursday, May 28th, 2026
item.description:
Tonight on Our News, legal proceedings begin following a tragic shooting in Bimini as a 12-year-old boy and a 40-year-old man face a magistrate’s court in connection with the incident. We investigate the growing debate over the latest national budget, from the Nurses Union’s concerns about crumbling clinics to Family Island residents questioning the timeline for promised fire equipment. Our team also brings you an update on the Ebola monitoring situation at Princess Margaret Hospital and the Finance Minister’s defense of controversial government contract practices. Join us for the full broadcast for these headlines, highlights from the Best of the Best music festival in Miami, and your complete weekend weather outlook.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.

Tonight on Our News, legal proceedings begin following a tragic shooting in Bimini as a 12-year-old boy and a 40-year-old man face a magistrate’s court in connection with the incident. We investigate the growing debate over the latest national budget, from the Nurses Union’s concerns about crumbling clinics to Family Island residents questioning the timeline for promised fire equipment. Our team also brings you an update on the Ebola monitoring situation at Princess Margaret Hospital and the Finance Minister’s defense of controversial government contract practices. Join us for the full broadcast for these headlines, highlights from the Best of the Best music festival in Miami, and your complete weekend weather outlook.

Tonight on Our News, legal proceedings begin following a tragic shooting in Bimini as a 12-year-old boy and a 40-year-old man face a magistrate’s court in connection with the incident. We investigate the growing debate over the latest national budget, from the Nurses Union’s concerns about crumbling clinics to Family Island residents questioning the timeline for promised fire equipment. Our team also brings you an update on the Ebola monitoring situation at Princess Margaret Hospital and the Finance Minister’s defense of controversial government contract practices. Join us for the full broadcast for these headlines, highlights from the Best of the Best music festival in Miami, and your complete weekend weather outlook.
